how_it_works: Unique items have fixed mods and are either found as drops, crafted from divination card sets, or obtained from specific boss kills. They range from leveling tools to mirror-tier endgame gear. Value is driven by build-enabling power, drop rate, and demand.
tips: Budget builds revolve around a single chase unique — plan the rest of the gear around enabling that item.
Mirror-tier uniques (Headhunter, Mageblood) are not required for most content — they enhance already-functional builds.
Many high-value uniques are league-specific or have limited drop sources — check trade site prices before farming for them.

items.name: Headhunter
items.source: Drop from rare monsters in T14+ maps; Maelström of Chaos (unique map) has increased chance
items.key_effect: When you kill a Rare monster, gain its mods for 20 seconds. Stacks up to 20 buffs simultaneously.
items.why_valuable: The best mapping belt in the game. Each rare kill snowballs into exponentially more power. Makes any build faster at map clearing.
items.notes: Price varies 60–200+ Divines per league depending on demand. Functionally strongest in dense maps with many rare monsters.

items.name: Mageblood
items.source: Drop from Uber Pinnacle bosses (Uber Sirus, Uber Maven, Uber Eater, Uber Exarch)
items.key_effect: Grants permanent uptime on all magic flasks without needing to press them. Effectively provides 4 always-active flask effects.
best_for: Any build that benefits from permanent flask effects
items.why_valuable: Completely removes flask piano management. Provides immunity to all ailments, movement speed, and other flask bonuses permanently.
items.notes: Often the most expensive item in the game. Enables a completely different playstyle — flask effects become passive bonuses.

items.name: Melding of the Flesh
items.source: Drop from Uber Pinnacle bosses
items.key_effect: Maximum Resistances cap at 90% (normally 75%). While all max resists are equal, you also gain +5 to all maximum resistances.
items.why_valuable: Allows 90%+ elemental resistances, dramatically reducing elemental damage taken. Build-defining for Elemental Aegis or max-res-stacking builds.
items.notes: Build-defining jewel. Requires all max resistances to be equal to get the bonus — constraining but extremely powerful when achieved.

items.name: Forbidden Flame / Forbidden Flesh
items.source: Drop from Uber Pinnacle bosses; pair must match (same ascendancy)
items.key_effect: Together grant the keystone from a specific ascendancy class (one jewel for each ascendancy). Effectively lets you take an ascendancy keystone without using your ascendancy points.
best_for: Builds that want a second ascendancy's keystone
items.why_valuable: Lets you 'borrow' powerful ascendancy keystones like Vaal Pact, Mistwalker, or Avatar of Fire without committing your ascendancy.
items.notes: Price depends heavily on which ascendancy is paired. Hinekora (Necro) and Chieftain pairs are most expensive. Requires both jewels to match.

items.name: The Squire
items.source: Drop from Heist targets; relatively rare
items.key_effect: Linked support gems in your off-hand also apply to your main hand weapon's gems. Effectively doubles the number of support gems for main skill.
items.why_valuable: Functionally gives your main skill up to 12 support gems. Massive DPS increase for builds with many support options.
items.notes: Most effective with builds that can fill the shield links with high-value supports. Less useful for builds with few strong support options.

items.name: Skin of the Lords
items.source: Drop from Corrupted Strongbox or any corrupted source
items.key_effect: 6-linked with a random colour combination. Grants a random keystone (which can be build-enabling or detrimental). No requirements to equip.
best_for: Builds that want specific keystones for free
items.why_valuable: The keystone it grants determines the value. 'Unnatural Strength' version is BiS for minion builds. Free 6-link base is also a budget alternative.
items.notes: Price varies enormously based on keystone. Corrupted to desired keystone. Unnatural Strength, Avatar of Fire, and Iron Reflexes versions are most sought.

items.name: The Annihilating Light
items.source: Drop from maps and bosses
items.key_effect: Grants +3 to all Spell Skill Gems. Hits deal 40% less damage. Spells deal 100% more damage.
best_for: Spellcasters with many spell gem levels
items.why_valuable: Massive spell gem level bonus and damage multiplier. The 40% less hit damage only matters for attack hit damage — pure spells get the full benefit.
items.notes: BiS weapon for many spellcaster archetypes. The 100% more damage makes it a top-tier damage weapon despite the downside.

items.name: Progenesis
items.source: Drop from Pinnacle bosses and high-tier maps
items.key_effect: 25% of Life loss from Hits is prevented and lost over 4 seconds instead. Substantial incoming damage mitigation.
items.why_valuable: Converts burst damage to DOT — lets you survive one-shot scenarios that would otherwise kill you. Extremely powerful defensive flask.
items.notes: The best defensive flask for life builds. Synergizes with high HP and regeneration to absorb burst hits that would otherwise be lethal.

items.name: Indigon
items.source: Drop from maps
items.key_effect: Spending mana increases spell damage. Caps at +200% increased spell damage for 4 seconds. Triggers at different spending thresholds.
items.why_valuable: Core item for Archmage and mana-stacking builds. The damage bonus can be enormous with high mana expenditure per second.
items.notes: Requires careful mana management. Most effective with Mana Flask for burst windows or high-sustain Archmage builds.

items.name: Clusters of Jewels (Megalomaniac / Large)
items.source: Drops from maps (all tiers for different sizes)
items.key_effect: 3-notable Megalomaniacs can combine specific notables inaccessible elsewhere (e.g. Unnatural Preservation + Renewal + Feasting Fiends for minions).
best_for: Any build seeking specific notable combos
items.why_valuable: Access to unique combinations of notables. Some triplet combos are best-in-slot for entire build archetypes.
items.notes: Price depends entirely on which notables are on the jewel. Rolling for specific notables requires many attempts or trade search.

items.name: Xoph's Blood
items.source: Drop from maps and bosses
items.key_effect: Avatar of Fire — converts 50% of physical/cold/lightning to fire. All damage converted to fire. Fire Penetration aura.
items.why_valuable: Build-enabling. Automatically converts all damage to fire, enabling full fire penetration without passive tree investment.
items.notes: Core item for Burning Arrow, Elemental Hit (fire), and any build wanting Avatar of Fire without taking the keystone.

items.name: Thread of Hope
items.source: Drop from maps and bosses
items.key_effect: Passives in a radius can be allocated without traveling the path to them. Radius size (small/medium/large/very large) determines reach.
best_for: Optimized builds reaching difficult keystone locations
items.why_valuable: Allows reaching isolated passive islands (Acrobatics, Unwavering Stance, remote keystones) without wasting 5–8 points on travel nodes.
items.notes: Value depends on the radius — very large radius enables the most efficient pathing. Often provides 5+ 'free' points on optimized builds.

items.name: Shaper's Touch
items.source: Drop from Shaper and maps
items.key_effect: Grants Armour and ES equal to Evasion Rating. Accuracy Rating = Life. Used in specific ES-stacking builds.
items.why_valuable: Enables a unique defensive profile where life directly increases accuracy — allowing high DPS through accuracy without accuracy investment elsewhere.
items.notes: Niche but powerful for specific builds. Requires the right defensive setup to benefit fully.

items.name: Kaom's Heart
items.source: Drop from maps and bosses
items.key_effect: +500 maximum Life. No linked sockets — body armour has 0 sockets. Massive flat life bonus.
items.why_valuable: Best flat life body armour in the game. The 0-socket downside only matters for skills that need body armour links.
items.notes: Price varies 10–40c typically. Excellent for RF, totems, and any life build that runs main skill from weapons or helm.

items.name: Tabula Rasa
items.source: Widely available from vendors and drops
items.key_effect: 6 white linked sockets. No stats. Allows any 6-link gem setup with any colours.
items.why_valuable: Cheap access to a 6-link at any point in the game. Removes the requirement to 6-link an armour piece.
items.notes: Standard league-start purchase. Usually 5–15c. Replaced once a real 6-link body armour is available.

items.name: Goldrim
items.source: Drop from any source; Vendor recipe: 3× Thaumetic Sulphite
items.key_effect: +36–40% to all Elemental Resistances. Massive resistance boost for leveling.
items.why_valuable: Eliminates resistance problems for all of leveling and early mapping. Allows using any armour in other slots.
items.notes: Usually 1–3c. Best-in-slot helmet for the first 50+ hours of every character if using item level restricted gear.

items.name: Oni-Goroshi
items.source: Drop from Hillock (Act 1) only — low drop rate
items.key_effect: Her Embrace — permanently take burning debuff but gain huge damage buff (20% more damage per second burning). Build-enabling for melee.
items.why_valuable: One of the best leveling weapons in the game if obtained. The damage bonus outweighs the self-burn at all levels until mapping.
items.notes: Getting the drop can take many Hillock resets. Once obtained, trivialises leveling for melee builds.

items.name: Pledge of Hands
items.source: Drop from maps
items.key_effect: +3 to Spell Skill Gems socketed in this staff. Free Spell Echo support linked to all socketed spells.
best_for: Any spell that benefits from Spell Echo
items.why_valuable: Free Spell Echo is extremely powerful. Also provides +3 gem levels for spell skill gems in the staff itself.
items.notes: Budget league-starter weapon. Usually 5–20c. Outclassed by wand pairs or endgame staves later, but strong for early red maps.

items.name: Cherrubim's Maleficence
items.source: Drop from maps and bosses
items.key_effect: 300–400 maximum Life. 100–150 maximum ES. 80–100% increased Chaos Damage. Significant chaos damage and defences.
items.why_valuable: Enables chaos-scaling damage with solid life and ES. Core item for budget chaos damage builds.
items.notes: Usually very affordable (1–5c). Excellent value for the stats provided for chaos builds in the first few weeks of a league.

items.name: Watcher's Eye
items.source: Drop from The Elder (Atlas boss encounter)
items.key_effect: Has 2–3 mods that enhance specific auras (Determination, Hatred, Haste, etc.) while those auras are active.
best_for: Any build running Hatred, Determination, or Grace
items.why_valuable: Can provide up to 3 powerful aura-specific bonuses in one jewel slot. Hatred variants (crit/cold pen) are most sought-after for DPS builds.
items.notes: Price varies enormously by mod combinations. Single-notable (budget) vs double or triple notable versions can differ 10× in price.
